questions.question: Find the determinant of the matrix $\begin{pmatrix} 4 & -2 \\ 3 & 5 \end{pmatrix}$.
questions.explanation: For a 2×2 matrix $\begin{pmatrix} a & b \\ c & d \end{pmatrix}$, the determinant is $ad - bc$. Here: $4(5) - (-2)(3) = 20 + 6 = 26$.

questions.question: Multiply the matrices $\begin{pmatrix} 2 & 1 \\ 3 & 0 \end{pmatrix} \times \begin{pmatrix} 4 \\ -1 \end{pmatrix}$. Express your answer as a column vector.
questions.explanation: First row: $2(4) + 1(-1) = 8 - 1 = 7$. Second row: $3(4) + 0(-1) = 12$. Result is $\begin{pmatrix} 7 \\ 12 \end{pmatrix}$.

questions.question: A company produces widgets using the production matrix $P = \begin{pmatrix} 8 & 3 \\ 5 & 6 \end{pmatrix}$ (rows are factories A and B; columns are products X and Y). In one week, factory A makes 8 units of X and 3 units of Y. Factory B makes 5 units of X and 6 units of Y. What is the total number of units of product X produced across both factories?
questions.explanation: The first column of matrix P represents product X. Total = 8 (factory A) + 5 (factory B) = 13 units.

questions.question: Find the determinant of $\begin{pmatrix} -3 & 6 \\ 2 & -4 \end{pmatrix}$.
questions.explanation: Determinant = $(-3)(-4) - (6)(2) = 12 - 12 = 0$. A determinant of 0 means the matrix is singular (not invertible).

questions.question: Compute $3 \times \begin{pmatrix} 2 & -1 \\ 0 & 4 \end{pmatrix}$.
questions.explanation: Scalar multiplication multiplies every element by 3: $\begin{pmatrix} 3(2) & 3(-1) \\ 3(0) & 3(4) \end{pmatrix} = \begin{pmatrix} 6 & -3 \\ 0 & 12 \end{pmatrix}$.
